title: tetragonia spicata var. laxa
intro: Tetragonia spicata var. laxa, also known as New Zealand spinach or kōka, is a perennial succulent plant native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its sprawling habit and edible leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Tetragonia spicata var. laxa include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water or combining them with a solvent such as ethanol.
